prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. After the federal elections and regional elections on May 2014, negotiations commenced to establish a new Federal Government and new regional governments: a Flemish, Walloon, French Community, and Brussels Government. A Government of the German-speaking Community was formed shortly after the elections. The Di Rupo Government was made up of the three traditional "ideological families": Flemish and French-speaking Christian democrats, liberals, and socialists. The Michel Government formed during these negotiations is a one-sided coalition of Flemish nationalists.
B. Following the simultaneous federal elections and regional elections of 25 May 2014, negotiations started to form a new Federal Government as well as new regional governments: a Flemish, Walloon, French Community and Brussels Government. A Government of the German-speaking Community was formed only a few days after the elections. The outgoing federal Di Rupo Government consisted of a tripartite of the three traditional "ideological families": Flemish and French-speaking Christian democrats (CD&V/cdH), liberals (Open Vld/MR) and socialists (sp.a/PS). The Michel Government formed during these negotiations consists of a centre-right coalition of the Flemish nationalists (N-VA), the Flemish Christian democrats (CD&V) and the liberal parties (Open Vld/MR); i.e. only one party at French-speaking side. The new Flemish Government reflects the same coalition, whereas the Walloon and French Community Governments are
C. After the federal and regional elections, which took place concurrently on May 25, 2014, discussions commenced regarding the establishment of a new Federal Government, in addition to new regional administrations for Flanders, Wallonia, the French Community, and Brussels. A Government for the German-speaking Community was constituted merely days subsequent to the electoral process. The preceding federal administration, known as the Di Rupo Government, comprised a three-party alliance representing the three established "ideological families": Christian democrats from both Flemish and French-speaking communities (CD&V/cdH), liberals (Open Vld/MR), and socialists (sp.a/PS). In contrast, the Michel Government, which emerged from these negotiations, is composed of a center-right alliance featuring Flemish nationalists (N-VA), Flemish Christian democrats (CD&V), and the liberal factions (Open Vld/MR); notably, this includes only a single party from the French-speaking community. The newly formed Flemish Government mirrors this exact coalition, while the Governments of Wallonia and the French Community are still being formed.

Answer:

options.A: After the federal elections and regional elections on May 2014, negotiations commenced to establish a new Federal Government and new regional governments: a Flemish, Walloon, French Community, and Brussels Government. A Government of the German-speaking Community was formed shortly after the elections. The Di Rupo Government was made up of the three traditional "ideological families": Flemish and French-speaking Christian democrats, liberals, and socialists. The Michel Government formed during these negotiations is a one-sided coalition of Flemish nationalists.
options.B: Following the simultaneous federal elections and regional elections of 25 May 2014, negotiations started to form a new Federal Government as well as new regional governments: a Flemish, Walloon, French Community and Brussels Government. A Government of the German-speaking Community was formed only a few days after the elections. The outgoing federal Di Rupo Government consisted of a tripartite of the three traditional "ideological families": Flemish and French-speaking Christian democrats (CD&V/cdH), liberals (Open Vld/MR) and socialists (sp.a/PS). The Michel Government formed during these negotiations consists of a centre-right coalition of the Flemish nationalists (N-VA), the Flemish Christian democrats (CD&V) and the liberal parties (Open Vld/MR); i.e. only one party at French-speaking side. options.C: After the federal and regional elections, which took place concurrently on May 25, 2014, discussions commenced regarding the establishment of a new Federal Government, in addition to new regional administrations for Flanders, Wallonia, the French Community, and Brussels. A Government for the German-speaking Community was constituted merely days subsequent to the electoral process. The preceding federal administration, known as the Di Rupo Government, comprised a three-party alliance representing the three established "ideological families": Christian democrats from both Flemish and French-speaking communities (CD&V/cdH), liberals (Open Vld/MR), and socialists (sp.a/PS). In contrast, the Michel Government, which emerged from these negotiations, is composed of a center-right alliance featuring Flemish nationalists (N-VA), Flemish Christian democrats (CD&V), and the liberal factions (Open Vld/MR); notably, this includes only a single party from the French-speaking community. The newly formed Flemish Government mirrors this exact coalition, while the Governments of Wallonia and the French Community are still being formed.
